title: Akamai Guardicore Agent Health
spl:
index=microseg sourcetype="guardicore:agent" earliest=-4h
| eval healthy=if(match(lower(status),"(?i)active|running|connected"),1,0)
| stats latest(healthy) as current_status latest(_time) as last_seen by agent_id, hostname, os
| where current_status=0 OR last_seen < relative_time(now(), "-2h")
| sort last_seen
description: Guardicore agents on workloads enforce segmentation policies. Offline or degraded agents leave workloads unprotected — effectively disabling zero trust enforcement on that asset.
value: Security operations teams monitor Guardicore agent health across all workloads, detecting disconnected agents that create segmentation gaps, tracking version compliance, and ensuring every workload maintains active microsegmentation enforcement.
implementation: Alert when agents go offline for >30 minutes on critical assets. Report on agent coverage (% of assets with active agents). Track agent version compliance.
visualization: Single value (offline agents), Table (unhealthy agents), Pie chart (agent status), Line chart (coverage trend).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Microseg blocks from new app deployments, missing exception rules, or planned segmentation rollouts.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Guardicore agent status events in `index=microseg` with `sourcetype=guardicore:syslog` or `sourcetype=guardicore:agent`. Key fields: `agent_id`, `agent_status` (Active/Inactive/Disconnected), `agent_version`, `hostname`, `os`, `label`, `last_communication`.
• Guardicore agents are installed on every workload to enforce microsegmentation. If an agent goes offline, that workload loses segmentation protection — it can communicate freely, bypassing all policies.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify agent status:
```spl
index=microseg earliest=-24h
| where isnotnull(agent_status)
| dedup hostname sortby -_time
| stats count by agent_status
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Agent health overview:
```spl
index=microseg earliest=-24h
| where isnotnull(agent_status)
| dedup hostname sortby -_time
| eval health=case(agent_status="Active", "Healthy", agent_status="Inactive", "WARNING", 1==1, "CRITICAL")
| stats count by health, os
```

Understanding this SPL: Shows agent health distribution by OS. Inactive agents may have stopped communicating (host powered off, agent crashed). Disconnected agents are a security gap — the workload is on the network without segmentation enforcement.

Agent version compliance:
```spl
index=microseg earliest=-24h
| where isnotnull(agent_version)
| dedup hostname sortby -_time
| stats count by agent_version
| sort agent_version
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) In Guardicore Centra: Assets tab > filter by agent status.
(b) Stop the Guardicore agent on a test host. Verify the status change.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Agent status distribution. Row 2 — Disconnected agents. Row 3 — Version distribution.
Alert: When agent on a critical workload goes inactive.

Runbook:
1. **Agent disconnected** — Check host. Restart agent service.
2. **Outdated agent** — Schedule update. Check compatibility.
3. **Many inactive** — May be a batch of VMs powered off. Verify with ops.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No agent status events** — Agent health may need to be enabled in Centra settings.
• **All agents show same version** — Auto-update may be enabled (good) or disabled (all stuck).
grandmaExplanation: Guardicore puts a tiny security agent on every server to enforce our network walls. If that agent stops working, the server loses its protection. We monitor every agent to make sure they are all running, because a single unprotected server can be the weak link an attacker exploits.
